info.name: Swiss Route Api
info.description: Backend REST API for planning and tracking public transport trips in Switzerland. Built with Java + Spring Boot + PostgreSQL, it acts as a business layer on top of the [Swiss Public Transport API](https://transport.opendata.ch/docs.html), allowing registered users to search connections, save favorite routes and stations, consult station boards, and keep a history of their planned trips.

item.name: Auth Service

request.description:
# 👤 User Registration

## 📌 Endpoint

**POST**  
`http://localhost:8080/api/users/register`

---

## 📖 Description

Registers a new user in the **SwissRoute API** platform.

This endpoint allows public user registration by providing basic account information such as name, email, password, and base city.

For security purposes:

- Passwords are encrypted using **BCrypt**
    
- Passwords must comply with strong security rules before being accepted
    

---

# 🔐 Access

**Public endpoint**  
No authentication required.

---

# 📥 Request

## Request Body (JSON)

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| name | String | ✅ Yes | Full name of the user |
| email | String | ✅ Yes | Unique email address |
| password | String | ✅ Yes | User password following security requirements |
| baseCity | String | ✅ Yes | User's base city |

---

## Password Requirements

The password must contain:

- Minimum **8 characters**
    
- At least:
    
    - One uppercase letter
        
    - One lowercase letter
        
    - One number
        
    - One special character
        

### Example of a valid password

``` text
Password123!

 ```

---

### Example Request

``` http
POST /api/users/register
Content-Type: application/json

 ```

``` json
{
  "name": "Joe Doe",
  "email": "joedoe@email.com",
  "password": "Password123!",
  "baseCity": "Madrid"
}

 ```

---

# 📤 Responses

---

## ✅ 201 Created

The user was successfully registered.

### Response Body (JSON)

``` json
{
    "name": "Joe Doe",
    "email": "joedoe@email.com",
    "baseCity": "Madrid",
    "createdAt": "2026-05-20T00:04:05.836Z"
}

 ```

---

## ❌ 400 Bad Request

The request contains invalid or missing data.

### Response Body (JSON)

``` json
{
    "code": 400,
    "name": "BAD_REQUEST",
    "description": "email: Email is required; name: Name is required; password: Password is required; baseCity: Base city is required",
    "timestamp": "2026-05-20T00:05:24.008Z"
}

 ```

### Possible Causes

- Missing required fields
    
- Invalid email format
    
- Password does not meet security requirements
    
- Malformed request body
    

---

## ⚠️ 409 Conflict

The email address is already registered.

### Response Body (JSON)

``` json
{
    "code": 409,
    "name": "CONFLICT",
    "description": "Email is already in use.",
    "timestamp": "2026-05-20T00:04:38.297Z"
}

 ```

# 🔐 User Login

## 📌 Endpoint

**POST**  
`/api/users/login`

---

## 📖 Description

Authenticates a registered user and returns a **JWT access token**.

This endpoint validates the provided credentials and generates a signed token that can be used to access protected resources within the **SwissRoute API** platform.

The returned token must be included in the `Authorization` header for authenticated requests.

---

# 🔐 Access

**Public endpoint**  
No authentication required.

---

# 📥 Request

## Request Body (JSON)

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| email | String | ✅ Yes | Registered user email |
| password | String | ✅ Yes | User password |

> ⚠️ All fields are required.  
Null, empty, or blank values are not allowed. 
  

---

### Example Request

``` http
POST /api/users/login
Content-Type: application/json

 ```

``` json
{
  "email": "joedoe@email.com",
  "password": "Password123!"
}

 ```

---

# 📤 Responses

---

## ✅ 200 OK

Authentication was successful.

### Response Body (JSON)

``` json
{
    "token": "eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiJ9.eyJzdWIiOiJkNTIxOGNiNC1iYjU3LTRlYTktYjAwMi1mMmU3MzUwNWQwNDEiLCJlbWFpbCI6ImpvZWRvZUBlbWFpbC5jb20iLCJpYXQiOjE3NzkzMjI2OTQsImV4cCI6MTc3OTMyNjI5NH0.GOV38Fwa9xDDNjqsFILinGixmLg2qLYi-_8J_ymTFrc",
    "tokenType": "Bearer",
    "expiresIn": 3600,
    "userId": "d5218cb4-bb57-4ea9-b002-f2e73505d041"
}

 ```

---

## ❌ 400 Bad Request

The request contains invalid or missing data.

### Response Body (JSON)

``` json
{
    "code": 400,
    "name": "BAD_REQUEST",
    "description": "password: Password is required; email: Email is required",
    "timestamp": "2026-05-21T00:18:38.544Z"
}

 ```

### Possible Causes

- Missing required fields
    
- Empty or blank values
    
- Malformed request body
    
- Invalid email format
    

---

## 🔐 401 Unauthorized

Authentication failed due to invalid credentials.

### Response Body (JSON)

``` json
{
    "code": 401,
    "name": "UNAUTHORIZED",
    "description": "Invalid credentials",
    "timestamp": "2026-05-21T00:19:05.308Z"
}

 ```

### Possible Causes

- Incorrect email
    
- Incorrect password
    
- User does not exist
    

---

💡 **Tip (security level 🔥):**  
The JWT token returned by this endpoint contains authenticated user information and is digitally signed to prevent tampering.

Protected endpoints should use the following header format:

``` http
Authorization: Bearer {{token}}

 ```

The token expires automatically after the configured expiration time (`expiresIn`).

item.description: Registration and access to the platform

request.description:
# 🚉 Search Stations

## 📌 Endpoint

**GET**  
`/api/stations`

---

## 📖 Description

Searches for train or transport stations using one of the following search methods:

- Station name (`query`)
    
- Geographic coordinates (`latitude` + `longitude`)
    

The endpoint integrates with the external transport provider and returns nearby or matching stations depending on the search type.

---

# 🔐 Access

This endpoint is **protected**.

Authentication is required using a **valid JWT token**.

### Authorization Header

``` http
Authorization: Bearer {{JWT}}

 ```

**Allowed roles:**

- Authenticated users
    

---

# 📥 Request

## Query Parameters

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| query | String | ❌ Conditional | Station name to search |
| latitude | Double | ❌ Conditional | Latitude coordinate |
| longitude | Double | ❌ Conditional | Longitude coordinate |

---

## Search Rules

### ✅ Search by Name

Use only:

- `query`
    

Example:

``` http
GET /api/stations?query=Basel

 ```

---

### ✅ Search by Coordinates

Use both:

- `latitude`
    
- `longitude`
    

Example:

``` http
GET /api/stations?latitude=47.3769&longitude=8.5417

 ```

---

## Validation Rules

### ⚠️ Coordinate Rules

- `latitude` and `longitude` must always be provided together
    
- Valid latitude range:
    
    - `-90` to `90`
        
- Valid longitude range:
    
    - `-180` to `180`
        

---

### ⚠️ Search Constraints

The following combinations are **not allowed**:

- Providing all parameters together (`query + latitude + longitude`)
    
- Sending only one coordinate
    
- Sending no search parameters
    

A request must use:

- Either `query`
    
- Or `latitude` + `longitude`
    

---

# 📤 Responses

---

## ✅ 200 OK

Stations were successfully retrieved.

---

### Response Example — Search by Name

``` json
{
    "stations": [
        {
            "id": "8500010",
            "name": "Basel SBB",
            "latitude": 47.547403,
            "longitude": 7.589564
        },
        {
            "id": "8500090",
            "name": "Basel Bad Bf",
            "latitude": 47.567301,
            "longitude": 7.606922
        }
    ]
}

 ```

---

### Response Example — Search by Coordinates

When searching by coordinates, each station includes the `distance` field.

``` json
{
    "stations": [
        {
            "id": null,
            "name": "Hauptbahnhof Zuerich, Zürich",
            "latitude": null,
            "longitude": null,
            "distance": 11
        }
    ]
}

 ```

---

## ❌ 400 Bad Request

The request contains invalid query parameters.

### Possible Causes

- Missing required search parameters
    
- Sending all parameters together
    
- Only one coordinate provided
    
- Blank `query`
    
- Invalid coordinate ranges
    
- Malformed query parameters
    

### Example Response

``` json
{
    "code": 400,
    "name": "BAD_REQUEST",
    "description": "latitude and longitude must be provided together",
    "timestamp": "2026-05-22T00:25:20.152Z"
}

 ```

---

## 🔐 401 Unauthorized

Authentication credentials were not provided or are invalid.

### Example Response

``` json
{
    "code": 401,
    "name": "UNAUTHORIZED",
    "description": "Authentication required or token expired",
    "timestamp": "2026-05-22T00:33:35.539Z"
}

 ```

### Possible Causes

- Missing JWT token
    
- Expired token
    
- Invalid token signature
    

---

## ⚠️ 404 Not Found

No stations were found matching the search criteria.

### Example Response

``` json
{
    "code": 404,
    "name": "NOT_FOUND",
    "description": "No stations found with the name: sagmade",
    "timestamp": "2026-05-22T00:34:33.657Z"
}

 ```

---

## 🔌 502 Bad Gateway

Error while communicating with the external transport provider.

### Example Response

``` json
{
    "code": 502,
    "name": "BAD_GATEWAY",
    "description": "Api Transport rejected the request",
    "timestamp": "2026-05-22T00:34:33.657Z"
}

 ```

### Possible Causes

- Invalid response from external API
    
- External provider processing error
    
- Request rejected by transport provider
    

---

## 🚨 503 Service Unavailable

The external transport service is currently unavailable.

### Example Response

``` json
{
    "code": 503,
    "name": "SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE",
    "description": "Api Transport is unavailable",
    "timestamp": "2026-05-22T00:34:33.657Z"
}

 ```

### Possible Causes

- External API downtime
    
- Network connectivity issues
    
- Timeout while contacting the provider
    

---

💡 **Tip (architecture level 🔥):**  
This endpoint supports both:

- Text-based station discovery
    
- Geolocation-based nearest station lookup
    

while enforcing strict validation rules to avoid ambiguous or inconsistent searches.

# 🚆 Search Connections Between Stations

## 📌 Endpoint

**GET**  
`/api/connections`

---

## 📖 Description

Retrieves available transport connections between two stations.

The endpoint supports optional filtering by:

- Date
    
- Time
    
- Transportation types
    

Each connection includes route details such as:

- Duration
    
- Products/services
    
- Route sections
    
- Departure and arrival information
    
- Platform details
    

The data is obtained from the external transport provider and normalized before being returned to authenticated users.

---

## 📖 Additional Behavior

Every successful connection search is persisted into the `search_history` database table for audit and personalization purposes.

The following information is stored:

| Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `user_id` | Authenticated user identifier extracted from JWT token |
| `origin` | Value provided in the `from` parameter |
| `destination` | Value provided in the `to` parameter |
| `resultCount` | Total number of connections returned by the API |
| `searchedAt` | Exact search timestamp in UTC format |

This allows the system to maintain a historical record of user searches for future features such as analytics, recommendations, or search history retrieval.

---

# 🔐 Access

This endpoint is **protected**.

Authentication is required using a **valid JWT token**.

### Authorization Header

``` http
Authorization: Bearer {{JWT}}

 ```

**Allowed roles:**

- Authenticated users
    

---

# 📥 Request

## Query Parameters

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| from | String | ✅ Yes | Origin station |
| to | String | ✅ Yes | Destination station |
| date | Date (`yyyy-MM-dd`) | ❌ No | Travel date |
| time | Time (`HH:mm`) | ❌ No | Desired departure time |
| transportations | Enum List | ❌ No | Transportation types filter |

---

## Supported Transportation Types

The `transportations` parameter accepts one or multiple values:

- `TRAIN`
    
- `TRAM`
    
- `SHIP`
    
- `BUS`
    
- `CABLEWAY`
    

---

## Example Request — Basic Search

``` http
GET /api/connections?from=Lausanne&to=Genève
Authorization: Bearer {{JWT}}

 ```

---

## Example Request — With Filters

``` http
GET /api/connections?from=Lausanne&to=Genève&date=2026-05-25&time=21:30&transportations=TRAIN,BUS
Authorization: Bearer {{JWT}}

 ```

---

# 📤 Responses

---

## ✅ 200 OK

Connections were successfully retrieved.

### Response Body (JSON)

``` json
{
    "connections": [
        {
            "origin": "Lausanne",
            "destination": "Genève",
            "duration": "00d00:54:00",
            "products": [
                "RE33"
            ],
            "sections": [
                {
                    "category": "RE",
                    "number": "33",
                    "operator": "SBB",
                    "destination": "Genève-Aéroport",
                    "departureStation": "Lausanne",
                    "departureTime": "2026-05-25T21:30:00Z",
                    "arrivalStation": "Genève",
                    "arrivalTime": "2026-05-25T22:24:00Z",
                    "platform": "7"
                }
            ]
        },
        {
            "origin": "Lausanne",
            "destination": "Genève",
            "duration": "00d00:49:00",
            "products": [
                "IR 95"
            ],
            "sections": [
                {
                    "category": "IR",
                    "number": "95",
                    "operator": "SBB",
                    "destination": "Genève",
                    "departureStation": "Lausanne",
                    "departureTime": "2026-05-25T21:50:00Z",
                    "arrivalStation": "Genève",
                    "arrivalTime": "2026-05-25T22:39:00Z",
                    "platform": "8"
                }
            ]
        }
    ]
}

 ```

---

## ❌ 400 Bad Request

The request contains invalid query parameters.

### Possible Causes

- Missing required parameters (`from`, `to`)
    
- Invalid date format
    
- Invalid time format
    
- Invalid transportation type
    
- Malformed query parameters
    

---

### Example — Missing Stations

``` json
{
    "code": 400,
    "name": "BAD_REQUEST",
    "description": "to: Destination station must not be blank; from: Origin station must not be blank",
    "timestamp": "2026-05-24T00:22:20.931Z"
}

 ```

---

### Example — Invalid Date

``` json
{
    "code": 400,
    "name": "BAD_REQUEST",
    "description": "Field 'date': invalid value '2026-05-25-34'",
    "timestamp": "2026-05-24T00:22:43.142Z"
}

 ```

---

### Example — Invalid Time

``` json
{
    "code": 400,
    "name": "BAD_REQUEST",
    "description": "Field 'time': invalid value '23:18:2321'",
    "timestamp": "2026-05-24T00:23:31.985Z"
}

 ```

---

### Example — Invalid Transportation Type

``` json
{
    "code": 400,
    "name": "BAD_REQUEST",
    "description": "Field 'transportations': invalid value 'buseta'",
    "timestamp": "2026-05-24T00:23:56.045Z"
}

 ```

---

## 🔐 401 Unauthorized

Authentication credentials were not provided or are invalid.

### Response Body (JSON)

``` json
{
  "code": 401,
  "name": "UNAUTHORIZED",
  "description": "Authentication required or token expired",
  "timestamp": "2026-05-22T00:33:35.539Z"
}

 ```

### Possible Causes

- Missing JWT token
    
- Expired token
    
- Invalid token signature
    

---

## ⚠️ 404 Not Found

No connections were found for the provided parameters.

### Response Body (JSON)

``` json
{
    "code": 404,
    "name": "NOT_FOUND",
    "description": "No connections found for the given parameters",
    "timestamp": "2026-05-24T00:24:29.585Z"
}

 ```

### User Not Found

``` json
{
  "code": 404,
  "name": "NOT_FOUND",
  "description": "user not found",
  "timestamp": "2026-05-24T00:24:29.585Z"
}

 ```

---

## 🔌 502 Bad Gateway

Error while communicating with the external transport provider.

### Response Body (JSON)

``` json
{
  "code": 502,
  "name": "BAD_GATEWAY",
  "description": "Api Transport rejected the request",
  "timestamp": "2026-05-22T00:34:33.657Z"
}

 ```

### Possible Causes

- Invalid response from transport API
    
- External provider processing error
    
- Request rejected by provider
    

---

## 🚨 503 Service Unavailable

The external transport provider is currently unavailable.

### Response Body (JSON)

``` json
{
  "code": 503,
  "name": "SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE",
  "description": "Api Transport is unavailable",
  "timestamp": "2026-05-22T00:34:33.657Z"
}

 ```

### Possible Causes

- External API downtime
    
- Network connectivity issues
    
- Timeout while contacting the provider
    

---

💡 **Tip (architecture level 🔥):**  
This endpoint centralizes route planning logic and abstracts the external transport provider, allowing clients to:

- Search connections consistently
    
- Filter transportation methods
    
- Retrieve normalized scheduling information
    
- Avoid direct dependency on external transport APIs

# 📚 Get Search History

## 📌 Endpoint

**GET**  
`/api/history?page=1&size=20`

---

## 📖 Description

Retrieves the paginated search history of the authenticated user.

Each record represents a previously executed connection search and includes:

- Origin station
    
- Destination station
    
- Number of results returned
    
- Search timestamp
    

The history is ordered by the most recent searches.

---

# 🔐 Access

This endpoint is **protected**.

Authentication is required using a **valid JWT token**.

### Authorization Header

``` http
Authorization: Bearer {{JWT}}

 ```

**Allowed roles:**

- Authenticated users
    

---

# 📥 Request

## Query Parameters

| Parameter | Type | Required | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| page | Integer | ❌ No | `1` | Page number (starts at `1`) |
| size | Integer | ❌ No | `20` | Number of items per page |

---

## Pagination Rules

- `page` must be greater than or equal to `1`
    
- `size` minimum value: `1`
    
- `size` maximum value: `50`
    

---

### Example Request

``` http
GET /api/history?page=1&size=20
Authorization: Bearer {{JWT}}

 ```

---

# 📤 Responses

---

## ✅ 200 OK

Search history was successfully retrieved.

### Response Body (JSON)

``` json
{
    "history": [
        {
            "id": "13fbb325-04cb-41a0-a5aa-9b61f55b66d3",
            "origin": "Lausanne",
            "destination": "Genève",
            "resultCount": 4,
            "searchedAt": "2026-05-25T20:57:38.072Z"
        },
        {
            "id": "5dabc735-6249-4ebc-9e94-39a1a73c3a56",
            "origin": "Lausanne",
            "destination": "Genève",
            "resultCount": 4,
            "searchedAt": "2026-05-25T20:57:28.993Z"
        }
    ],
    "page": 1,
    "size": 20,
    "totalElements": 2,
    "totalPages": 1
}

 ```

---

## ❌ 400 Bad Request

The request contains invalid pagination parameters.

### Example Response

``` json
{
    "code": 400,
    "name": "BAD_REQUEST",
    "description": "page: Page must be greater than or equal to 1; size: Size must be less than or equal to 50",
    "timestamp": "2026-05-25T20:58:26.857Z"
}

 ```

### Possible Causes

- Invalid page number
    
- Invalid page size
    
- Negative values
    
- Size greater than allowed limit
    

---

## 🔐 401 Unauthorized

Authentication credentials were not provided or are invalid.

### Example Response

``` json
{
  "code": 401,
  "name": "UNAUTHORIZED",
  "description": "Authentication required or token expired",
  "timestamp": "2026-05-25T00:33:35.539Z"
}

 ```

### Possible Causes

- Missing JWT token
    
- Expired token
    
- Invalid token signature
    

---

## ⚠️ 404 Not Found

The authenticated user was not found.

### Example Response

``` json
{
  "code": 404,
  "name": "NOT_FOUND",
  "description": "User not found",
  "timestamp": "2026-05-25T00:34:33.657Z"
}

 ```

# 🗑️ Delete Search History Item

## 📌 Endpoint

**DELETE**  
`/api/history/{ITEM_ID}`

---

## 📖 Description

Deletes a specific search history item belonging to the authenticated user.

Only the owner of the history entry can delete it.

---

# 🔐 Access

This endpoint is **protected**.

Authentication is required using a **valid JWT token**.

### Authorization Header

``` http
Authorization: Bearer {{JWT}}

 ```

**Allowed roles:**

- Authenticated users
    

---

# 📥 Request

## Path Parameters

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| ITEM_ID | UUID | ✅ Yes | Unique identifier of the history item |

---

### Example Request

``` http
DELETE /api/history/13fbb325-04cb-41a0-a5aa-9b61f55b66d3
Authorization: Bearer {{JWT}}

 ```

---

# 📤 Responses

---

## ✅ 204 No Content

The history item was successfully deleted.

---

## ❌ 400 Bad Request

The provided path parameter is invalid.

### Example Response

``` json
{
    "code": 400,
    "name": "BAD_REQUEST",
    "description": "Invalid path parameter for: 'itemId'",
    "timestamp": "2026-05-25T20:44:37.866Z"
}

 ```

### Possible Causes

- Invalid UUID format
    
- Malformed path parameter
    

---

## 🔐 401 Unauthorized

Authentication credentials were not provided or are invalid.

### Example Response

``` json
{
  "code": 401,
  "name": "UNAUTHORIZED",
  "description": "Authentication required or token expired",
  "timestamp": "2026-05-22T00:33:35.539Z"
}

 ```

---

## ⚠️ 404 Not Found

The specified history item does not exist.

### Example Response

``` json
{
  "code": 404,
  "name": "NOT_FOUND",
  "description": "Item id not found",
  "timestamp": "2026-05-24T00:24:29.585Z"
}

 ```

# 🧹 Clear Search History

## 📌 Endpoint

**DELETE**  
`/api/history`

---

## 📖 Description

Deletes the entire search history of the authenticated user.

This operation permanently removes all stored connection searches associated with the user account.

---

# 🔐 Access

This endpoint is **protected**.

Authentication is required using a **valid JWT token**.

### Authorization Header

``` http
Authorization: Bearer {{JWT}}

 ```

**Allowed roles:**

- Authenticated users
    

---

# 📤 Responses

---

## ✅ 204 No Content

The search history was successfully cleared.

---

## 🔐 401 Unauthorized

Authentication credentials were not provided or are invalid.

### Example Response

``` json
{
  "code": 401,
  "name": "UNAUTHORIZED",
  "description": "Authentication required or token expired",
  "timestamp": "2026-05-22T00:33:35.539Z"
}

 ```

### Possible Causes

- Missing JWT token
    
- Expired token
    
- Invalid token signature
    

---

## ⚠️ 404 Not Found

The authenticated user was not found.

### Example Response

``` json
{
  "code": 404,
  "name": "NOT_FOUND",
  "description": "user not found",
  "timestamp": "2026-05-24T00:24:29.585Z"
}

 ```

# ⭐ Register Favorite Route

## 📌 Endpoint

**POST**  
`/api/favorite-routes`

---

## 📖 Description

Registers a favorite route for the authenticated user.

Favorite routes allow users to quickly save and reuse frequently searched routes such as:

- Home to work
    
- Daily commute
    
- Frequent travel destinations
    

Each favorite route is associated with the authenticated user and includes:

- Route name
    
- Origin station
    
- Destination station
    
- Preferred transportation type (optional)
    

The route name must be unique in the system.

---

# 🔐 Access

This endpoint is **protected**.

Authentication is required using a **valid JWT token**.

### Authorization Header

``` http
Authorization: Bearer {{JWT}}

 ```

**Allowed roles:**

- Authenticated users
    

---

# 📥 Request

## Request Body (JSON)

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| name | String | ✅ Yes | Unique favorite route name |
| origin | String | ✅ Yes | Origin station |
| destination | String | ✅ Yes | Destination station |
| transportationType | Enum | ❌ No | Preferred transportation type |

---

## Supported Transportation Types

The `transportationType` field accepts the following values:

- `TRAIN`
    
- `TRAM`
    
- `SHIP`
    
- `BUS`
    
- `CABLEWAY`
    

The value is case-insensitive.

---

## Example Request

``` http
POST /api/favorite-routes
Authorization: Bearer {{JWT}}
Content-Type: application/json

 ```

``` json
{
  "name": "Home to work",
  "origin": "Geneve",
  "destination": "Zurich",
  "transportationType": "TRAIN"
}

 ```

---

# 📤 Responses

---

## ✅ 201 Created

The favorite route was successfully created.

### Response Body (JSON)

``` json
{
    "id": "028abc30-423b-4b17-8bb8-87647d860b6a",
    "name": "Home to work",
    "origin": "Geneve",
    "destination": "Zurich",
    "transportType": "TRAIN",
    "createdAt": "2026-05-26T23:58:17.137Z"
}

 ```

---

## ❌ 400 Bad Request

The request contains invalid or missing fields.

### Possible Causes

- Missing required fields
    
- Blank values
    
- Invalid transportation type
    
- Malformed request body
    

### Example Response

``` json
{
    "code": 400,
    "name": "BAD_REQUEST",
    "description": "name: Name is required; origin: Origin is required",
    "timestamp": "2026-05-26T23:59:10.245Z"
}

 ```

---

## 🔐 401 Unauthorized

Authentication credentials were not provided or are invalid.

### Possible Causes

- Missing JWT token
    
- Expired token
    
- Malformed token
    
- Invalid token signature
    

### Example Response

``` json
{
    "code": 401,
    "name": "UNAUTHORIZED",
    "description": "Authentication required or token expired",
    "timestamp": "2026-05-26T23:59:58.731Z"
}

 ```

---

## ⚠️ 404 Not Found

The authenticated user was not found in the system.

### Example Response

``` json
{
    "code": 404,
    "name": "NOT_FOUND",
    "description": "User not registered",
    "timestamp": "2026-05-27T00:00:14.117Z"
}

 ```

---

## ⚠️ 409 Conflict

The favorite route name already exists.

### Possible Causes

- Duplicate route name
    
- Unique constraint violation
    

### Example Response

``` json
{
    "code": 409,
    "name": "CONFLICT",
    "description": "Favorite route name already exists",
    "timestamp": "2026-05-27T00:01:20.552Z"
}

 ```
